text: Caffeine supplementation (3-9 mg/kg) improves time to exhaustion in endurance running with a medium effect size
Caffeine improves endurance running time trial performance with a small but significant effect size compared to placebo
Caffeine has a medium ergogenic effect on time to exhaustion in recreational runners
Caffeine has a medium ergogenic effect on time to exhaustion in trained runners
Effective caffeine doses for endurance running performance range between 3 and 9 mg/kg body mass
The ergogenic effect of caffeine on endurance running is insufficiently studied in women, limiting evidence-based recommendations for female runners
